extractions.problem: Understanding how endothelial cells collectively organize into vascular networks and sprouts in vitro through mechanical cell-ECM interactions, bridging single-cell behavior to collective morphogenesis.
extractions.method: Hybrid cellular Potts model (CPM) coupled with finite element method (FEM) to simulate cell motility, ECM mechanics, and mechanosensing (durotaxis, strain stiffening, cell traction).
extractions.finding: Three biologically plausible assumptions — cell traction, strain stiffening, and durotaxis — suffice to reproduce endothelial cell behavior across three spatial scales: single cells, pairwise interactions, and collective network formation/sprouting.
extractions.dataset: In vitro endothelial cells on compliant matrices (H5V murine embryonic heart endothelial cells, bovine aortic endothelial cells on acrylamide gels)
extractions.metric: Cell length, cell orientation, spreading area, qualitative comparison to experimental morphology
extractions.baseline: Published experimental observations of endothelial cells on compliant substrates
extractions.result: Single parameter set reproduces behavior across three spatial scales with isotropic orientation response to applied strain

extractions.problem: Exact stochastic simulation of large biochemical networks is computationally prohibitive, limiting the study of single-cell dynamics in realistic biological systems.
extractions.method: Hybrid jump/continuous Markov stochastic simulation (HyJCMSS) with adaptive Euler-Maruyama and Milstein integrators, parallelized via MPI for supercomputers.
extractions.finding: Hy3S enables efficient and accurate stochastic simulation of large biochemical networks with thousands of reactions, including bistable networks with spontaneous state transitions.
extractions.dataset: Nonlinear cycle test, large-scale benchmark (up to 20,000 reactions), bistable multiscale biochemical network with positive feedback
extractions.metric: Error in probability distribution, mean, and variance vs system size; computational time
extractions.result: Scales to 20,000 reactions; accuracy improves with system size; open-source GPL-licensed

extractions.problem: The mechanisms by which unconventional 'virtual memory' CD8+ T cells develop in the absence of antigen-driven activation, and the role of Eomesodermin (Eomes) regulation in this process.
extractions.method: Flow cytometry, adoptive transfer, bone marrow chimeras, and in vivo infection models using knockout mice (IFNAR−/−, IRF9−/−, STAT1−/−, Eomes conditional KO) with polyI:C stimulation and Listeria infection.
extractions.finding: Type I interferon signaling directly activates Eomes expression in CD8+ T cells through the ISGF3 complex (STAT1/2/IRF9), driving the development, function, and age-dependent expansion of virtual memory CD8+ T cells.
extractions.dataset: C57BL/6 and BALB/c mice, IFNAR−/−, IRF9−/−, STAT1−/− knockout strains, 6-10 week old
extractions.metric: Cell frequency, absolute counts, Eomes MFI, mRNA expression levels
extractions.result: Significant reduction of VM CD8+ T cells and Eomes expression in IFNAR−/− mice; IFNβ-induced Eomes upregulation is IRF9- and STAT1-dependent

extractions.problem: HTS analysis projects frequently deviate from standard workflows, requiring custom scripts, but no unified Python framework existed for rapid development across common HTS data formats.
extractions.method: Python library (HTSeq) providing parsers for common HTS formats (SAM/BAM, FASTQ, GFF, VCF, BED), genomic interval data structures, and the htseq-count tool for RNA-Seq read counting.
extractions.finding: HTSeq enables rapid development of custom HTS analysis scripts by integrating parsers for many common formats; htseq-count correctly handles ambiguous read-gene overlaps for differential expression preprocessing.
extractions.dataset: General-purpose tool for high-throughput sequencing data (RNA-Seq demonstrated)
extractions.result: 1.2 million reads/minute (~0.6M read pairs), ~250 MB RAM on standard laptop

extractions.problem: The gap between known protein sequences and experimentally determined structures remains large, and existing homology modelling tools needed improved accuracy and support for protein complexes.
extractions.method: Automated homology modelling with ProMod3 engine using BLAST/HHblits template search, fragment-based loop modelling, TreePack side-chain optimization, CHARMM27 energy minimization via OpenMM, and QMEANDisCo local quality estimation.
extractions.finding: ProMod3 significantly improves model accuracy over the previous ProMod-II engine; SWISS-MODEL now supports homo- and heteromeric complex modelling with stoichiometry inference.
extractions.metric: lDDT (Local Distance Difference Test), GDT-HA, TM-score
extractions.result: Significantly improved lDDT, GDT-HA, and TM-score vs ProMod-II on 250 CAMEO targets

extractions.problem: Mammalian organogenesis involves rapid transformation of germ layers into organs, but a comprehensive single-cell transcriptional atlas of this process was lacking.
extractions.method: sci-RNA-seq3 (single-cell combinatorial indexing) profiling ~2 million cells from 61 mouse embryos E9.5–E13.5, with trajectory inference via Monocle 3.
extractions.finding: Generated the Mouse Organogenesis Cell Atlas (MOCA) identifying >500 cell subtypes and 56 developmental trajectories spanning essentially every organ system, with thousands of marker genes.
extractions.dataset: ~2 million cells from 61 C57BL/6 mouse embryos, E9.5–E13.5
extractions.metric: Number of cell subtypes, trajectories, and marker genes; UMIs per cell
extractions.baseline: Previous single-cell studies of mid-gestational mouse development
extractions.result: 2,058,652 cells profiled, 500+ cell subtypes, 56 trajectories, primordial germ cells detected at 0.01% frequency

extractions.problem: Clinical responses to cancer drugs are strongly influenced by genomic alterations, but no large-scale public resource existed to systematically link drug sensitivity to molecular markers for biomarker discovery.
extractions.method: High-throughput drug sensitivity screening across cancer cell lines integrated with genomic data (somatic mutations, copy number, transcriptomics) from the COSMIC database, with statistical association analysis.
extractions.finding: GDSC provides the largest public resource for cancer drug sensitivity data integrated with genomic markers, facilitating preclinical identification of therapeutic biomarkers.
extractions.dataset: ~700 cancer cell lines, 138 anticancer drugs, ~75,000 drug sensitivity experiments
extractions.metric: Drug sensitivity (IC50), statistical associations between genomic features and drug response
extractions.result: 75,000 experiments across 700 cell lines and 138 drugs; freely available at cancerRxgene.org

extractions.problem: Single-cell RNA-seq data manifolds contain both discrete cell types and continuous transitions, but existing methods cannot robustly represent both in a scalable, interpretable map.
extractions.method: Partition-based graph abstraction (PAGA) using kNN graph construction, Louvain clustering, and a statistical connectivity measure between partitions to generate topology-preserving maps at multiple resolutions.
extractions.finding: PAGA reconciles clustering with trajectory inference, producing consistent topology across multiple datasets and scaling to millions of cells with much higher computational efficiency than single-cell-level analysis.
extractions.dataset: 4 hematopoietic datasets, adult planaria, zebrafish embryo, 1.3 million mouse brain neurons
extractions.metric: Topology preservation, computational efficiency, cross-dataset consistency
extractions.baseline: Existing manifold learning and trajectory inference methods (diffusion maps, force-directed graphs)
extractions.result: Consistent topology across 4 hematopoietic datasets; benchmarked on 1 million neurons; much higher computational efficiency

extractions.problem: Mechanisms controlling lung progenitor differentiation into mature alveolar cell types are incompletely known due to limited lineage markers and ensemble averaging in bulk transcriptomics.
extractions.method: Microfluidic single-cell RNA-seq on 198 cells from mouse distal lung epithelium at 4 developmental stages (E14.5, E16.5, E18.5, adult), with unbiased genome-wide cell classification.
extractions.finding: Confirmed the classical model of distal lung epithelial diversity, discovered many novel cell-type markers including transcriptional regulators, and reconstructed the full life cycle of the AT2 cell lineage from bipotential progenitors.
extractions.dataset: 198 cells from mouse distal lung epithelium (C57BL/6, E14.5–adult)
extractions.metric: Genome-wide transcriptional profiles, marker gene specificity
extractions.baseline: Classical model of epithelial cell-type diversity in distal lung based on marker expression and fate-mapping
extractions.result: Identified distinct cell populations and novel markers; reconstructed bipotential progenitor maturation along both alveolar lineages

extractions.problem: Pooled CRISPR screens are restricted to simple readouts (proliferation, sortable markers), while arrayed screens with transcriptome profiling have much lower throughput.
extractions.method: CROP-seq re-engineers the lentiviral LentiGuide-Puro vector to place the gRNA cassette in the 3' LTR, making it part of a polyadenylated mRNA detectable by droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq.
extractions.finding: CROP-seq enables pooled CRISPR screens with single-cell transcriptome resolution, maintaining high genome editing efficiency and compatibility with standard cloning protocols.
extractions.dataset: K562, Jurkat, HEK293T cell lines; T cell stimulation screen targeting TCR signaling regulators
extractions.metric: Genome editing efficiency, functional viral titer, out-of-frame indel frequency
extractions.baseline: LentiGuide-Puro (standard pooled CRISPR vector); Perturb-seq, CRISP-seq
extractions.result: 90.5% genome editing efficiency (vs 95.5% LentiGuide-Puro), 62.4% out-of-frame indels, comparable functional titers
